sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 19.19 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.19)

statement.latex: A finite transitive permutation group$G$is said to have the if, given any orbit$O$of$G$on$2$-sets, and any proper block of imprimitivity for$G$acting on$O$, the graph with edge set$O B$is connected. Such a group must be primitive, and basic (not contained in a wreath product with the product action); it cannot have an imprimitive subgroup of index~$2$.
